title: GS Retail is expected to continue its profitability improvement this year as consumer sentiment reco.. - 매일경제
content:
GS Retail is expected to continue its profitability improvement this year as consumer sentiment recovers and the convenience store industry's intensive restructuring is combined. In response, Hanwha Investment & Securities raised its performance forecast and target stock price of GS Retail. Hanwha Investment & Securities raised its target stock price of GS Retail from 27,000 won to 30,000 won in a report published on the 6th. GS Retail's stock price has rebounded more than 50% in the past year, reaching 21,450 won (as of the closing price on the 3rd). According to Hanwha Investment & Securities, GS Retail's consolidated sales in the first quarter of this year are expected to rise 2.9% year-on-year to KRW 2.84 trillion and its operating profit to KRW 50 billion, a 29.6% jump, in line with market expectations. In particular, while maintaining a conservative opening stance in the convenience store sector, which is its main business, and suppressing the increase in fixed costs, the growth rate of existing stores is expected to improve significantly, recording 4%. In addition, the quality growth of surviving stores is expected as the closure of sluggish stores continues in the convenience store industry. According to the Ministry of Trade, Industry and Energy, the number of convenience store stores fell 2.9% in February compared to the same month last year. While lower-tier operators are closing sluggish stores, sales per store of the top-tier stores that survived are on the rise. Lee Jin-hyeop, a researcher at Hanwha Investment & Securities, said, "With quantitative growth limited, qualitative growth is expected to continue and the leverage effect of convenience store operations is expected to begin in earnest. This is the path that Japanese convenience stores walked 10 years ago, and the stock price of Japanese convenience stores was reevaluated at the time." It is predicted that competitors will continue to close their sluggish stores. Researcher Lee said, "Korea Seven's sales fell 9% last year compared to the previous year, but its operating deficit has been reduced, but it is highly likely to maintain its existing strategy as its annual operating deficit still reaches 68.6 billion won. E-Mart 24 is also in a conservative situation." In addition to convenience stores, other business units that have been hampering their performance are also gradually narrowing their deficits through the efficiency process. Super sectors such as GS the Fresh are also expected to see their existing store growth turn positive in the first quarter of this year. ※ This article was translated using AI technology for reader convenience. title: Appalachian State Women’s Tennis Downs Georgia Southern - WHKY
content:
The App State tennis team swept Georgia Southern 4-0 on Friday, marking head coach Hannah Fetters’ 100th career victory. Alexi Cleveland clinched the match for the Mountaineers (9-10) with a 6-3, 6-2 straight-set win at No. 5 singles for her fourth win of the season. At No. 1 singles, No. 9 Savannah Dada-Mascoll extended her winning streak to a career-best 17 matches with a 6-3, 6-1 victory. The Yorkshire, England, native has now won 20 consecutive sets, not dropping one since Feb. 28. Junior Taya Powell snapped a three-match losing streak with a 6-3, 6-2 win at No. 2, putting App State within one point of the overall dual-match victory. The Mountaineers opened play by dominating doubles, holding leads on all three courts and clinching the point with wins at Nos. 1 and 2. The No. 1 pair of Ruby Broadbent and Dada-Mascoll finished first, earning their second straight win as a duo. Naledi Manyube and Powell then clinched the doubles point at No. 2 in their first win together since Feb. 7 against Western Carolina. Carrying a 1-0 lead into singles play, the Mountaineers led the first set on four courts. Dada-Mascoll finished first at the No. 1 spot to double the lead. Powell maintained a steady advantage in both sets against Georgia Southern (7-10) to finish second. Cleveland, playing at No. 5 and leading 3-2 in the first set, took three of the next four games to win 6-3 before cruising through the second set to clinch the overall match. The Mountaineers travel to Conway, S.C. to face Coastal Carolina on Saturday. First serve at the Stevens Tennis Complex is scheduled for 10 a.m. Results:

title: GS Caltex's '0% Rebellion' New History of Spring Volleyball' for the first time in '6 games'... Lee Young-taek said, "Let's just go to spring volleyball." We're on the same page [the scene of Jangchung] - 스타뉴스
content:
This is an unexpected scenario even for the head coach who achieved a miracle. GS Caltex regained the throne for the first time in five years with an unprecedented record of winning all six games of spring volleyball. GS Caltex beat Korea Expressway Corporation with a set score of 3-1 in Game 3 of the women's championship match of the 2025-26 Jin Air V-League at Jangchung Gymnasium in Seoul on the 5th and lifted the trophy for the first time in five years. As a result, GS Caltex became the first team in the history of the V-League to become a champion through semi-PO. In addition, it is the first time in history that the third-place team in the regular league has won all three championships. It was a perfect 'break the seal'. After winning a semi-PO against Heungkuk Life Insurance, which was a single match, he defeated Hyundai Engineering & Construction with two consecutive wins in the PO. GS Caltex, which has reached its peak, defeated even the top-ranked road corporation in the regular league with all three games in the championship match. He achieved a "flawless championship" that never lost in the tough spring volleyball matchup. Even GS Caltex coach Lee Young-taek said, "I didn't expect it at all because there were many variables such as injuries at the beginning of the season. It was a win that no one expected until the spring volleyball match, as he confessed, "First of all, let's go to spring volleyball." The driving force behind the miracle was the presence of ace Giselle Silva and the belief of the players. Coach Lee Young-taek said, "I thought it would be worth trying in a short-term game because Silva has an ace, and Silva did it for me as expected," adding, "I'm the same, but the players have grown a lot." Another reason why this victory is valuable is that it has overcome the relationship between natural enemies and the Korea Expressway Corporation. GS Caltex was overwhelmingly inferior to Korea Expressway Corporation with 1 win and 5 losses in the regular league this season, and suffered the humiliation of all three losses, especially in the away game in Gimcheon. However, the game has changed on the most important stage. GS Caltex beat the spirit of the Korea Expressway Corporation by catching the first and second Champs in a row in Gimcheon's enemy territory. And it had a spectacular finale in Jangchung, the home room. Even when he barely managed to secure third place by chasing at the end of the regular season, few predicted GS Caltex's victory. Due to the severe physical burden, there were only three cases in which the third-ranked team in the regular league won the V-League. Moreover, there has never been a case where a team that has reached the semi-PO has won a championship. However, GS Caltex changed its slim chance to a miracle of 100 percent. It was a "0% rebellion". <© STARNEWS. All rights reserved. title: GS Engineering Sells 1,780 Units Near Daejeon's Yonggye Station - 조선일보
content: Part of Doan New Town's second phase, near future tram line and expanding infrastructure GS Engineering & Construction is proceeding with the sale of ‘Doan Jai Centum Rich,’ a residential complex under construction at 267-3, Yonggye-dong, Yuseong-gu, Daejeon. The complex spans from basement level 2 to a maximum of 42 floors above ground, comprising 2,293 households. Of these, 1,780 units will be offered for general sale. The first phase includes 841 units of 84㎡ exclusive area and 105 units of 99㎡. The second phase consists of 626 units of 84㎡, 206 units of 115㎡, and 2 units of 134㎡. Over 80% of the total units are allocated to the 84㎡ size, which is in high demand. Doan Jai Centum Rich will be supplied as the second phase of Doan New Town development nears completion. Doan New Town, a large-scale residential district spanning Seo-gu and Yuseong-gu in Daejeon, has seen rising housing demand as key infrastructure was established through its first and second phases. Once the third phase is finalized, it is expected to emerge as a representative emerging residential area in southwestern Daejeon. The complex benefits from a prime location near the planned Yonggye Station of Daejeon Metro Line 2 Tram, accessible within a 5-minute walk. Once the line opens in 2028, travel to Yuseong Hot Springs Station will take approximately 10 minutes, and to Government Complex Station, around 21 minutes. Alongside the tram, the expansion of the Fourth Ring Road is planned, which is expected to significantly enhance regional accessibility once the project is fully underway. Educational facilities are also well-established. An elementary school and high school are planned near the first phase, while a middle school is under development around the second phase. Additionally, residents can access lifestyle amenities such as Lotte Mart, NC Department Store, Lotte Cinema, and Konyang University Hospital. A central park spanning approximately 350,000 square meters is also planned south of the complex. Ongoing development projects in the vicinity are anticipated to continuously expand living infrastructure. In March, construction began on the Southwest Comprehensive Sports Town in Hakhwa-dong, Yuseong-gu, covering about 760,000 square meters with an investment of 1.4 trillion won. The project includes sports facilities, parks, public institutions, and residential areas, alongside plans for a national semiconductor and nano-industry complex. Doan Jai Centum Rich incorporates specialized designs reflecting current trends. It adopts a 4-bay slab-type structure with south-facing layouts to optimize natural light and ventilation. Alpha rooms and dressing rooms enhance space utility, while piloti designs on the first floor of each building ensure openness and privacy for low-level units. Community facilities are diverse. The resident-exclusive ‘Club Jai-an’ features a golf practice range, fitness club, sauna, cafeteria, GX room, reading room, and guesthouse. Childcare facilities, including a daycare center and kids’ library, are also provided. A sales official stated, “Doan Jai Centum Rich is a large-scale Jai-brand complex supplied in the core area of Doan New Town. As the final large-scale sale of the second phase, it holds rarity and is expected to become a residential hub in the region once the third phase is completed.” Meanwhile, the model house for Doan Jai Centum Rich will be located at 667-6, Yonggye-dong, Yuseong-gu, Daejeon.

title: GS Caltex Claims Undefeated Title, Historic Third-Place Victory - 조선일보
content: Defeating Heungkuk Life, Hyundai Engineering & Construction, Korea Expressway Corporation in six straight postseason matches, ending five-year title drought [OSEN=Hong Ji-su Reporter] GS Caltex, which finished third in the regular season, achieved an 'undefeated' championship in the postseason. GS Caltex won the 3rd match of the 2025-2026 V-League Women's Division Championship Decider held at Jangchung Gymnasium on the 5th with a set score of 3-1 (25-15, 19-25, 25-20, 25-20). Silva scored 36 points, Kwon Min-ji 15 points, and Oh Se-yeon 11 points. Korea Expressway Corporation’s Moma scored 18 points and 15 points but was no match for GS Caltex’s firepower. GS Caltex, which advanced to the championship decider by defeating Heungkuk Life in the preliminary playoffs and Hyundai Engineering & Construction in the playoffs, ascended to the top without conceding a single match to Korea Expressway Corporation. GS Caltex played a total of six matches from the preliminary playoffs to the championship decider without a single loss. In the preliminary playoffs against Heungkuk Life on the 24th of last month, they secured a 3-1 set score victory and advanced to the playoffs. In the 1st playoff match against Hyundai Engineering & Construction on the 25th, they won 3-1, and in the 2nd match on the 28th, they won 3-0, successfully advancing to the championship decider. They also won all matches against Korea Expressway Corporation. In the 1st match, they won 3-1, and in the 2nd match on the 3rd, they won 3-2. GS Caltex concluded the series in the 3rd match. Unlike Korea Expressway Corporation, which advanced directly to the championship decider as the regular league leader, GS Caltex played their 6th postseason match on that day. Physical stamina was a variable, but the GS Caltex team, riding high on momentum, overcame the physical challenges. They reclaimed the title for the first time in five years since the 2020-2021 season. GS Caltex has now lifted their fourth championship trophy in the women’s professional volleyball championship decider, following the 2007-2008, 2013-2014, and 2020-2021 seasons. Starting from 3rd place, GS Caltex set a historic record as the first team to start from the preliminary playoffs and win the championship decider, as well as the first 3rd-place team to achieve an undefeated championship.

title: GS Caltex sweeps V League postseason, secures unbeaten championship - CHOSUNBIZ - Chosunbiz
content: GS Caltex, which finished third in the regular season, achieved an undefeated postseason championship. GS Caltex beat its opponent 3-1 (25-15 19-25 25-20 25-20) in the third game of the 2025-2026 V League women's championship held at Jangchung Arena on the 5th. Silva scored 36 points, Kwon Min-ji scored 15, and Oh Se-yeon scored 11. For Korea Expressway Corporation, Moma scored 18 points and another player scored 15, but they were no match for GS Caltex's firepower. Having beaten Heungkuk Life in the quarterfinal, and Hyundai Engineering & Construction in the playoff, GS Caltex advanced to the championship without losing a single game to Korea Expressway Corporation and claimed the title. GS Caltex played six games from the quarterfinal to the championship without losing once. On the 24th of the previous month in the quarterfinal against Heungkuk Life, they won 3-1 in sets to secure a playoff ticket. On the 25th, in playoff game 1 against Hyundai Engineering & Construction, they won 3-1 in sets, and on the 28th, they won game 2 by 3-0 in sets to advance to the championship. They also won both matches against Korea Expressway Corporation. GS Caltex, which won game 1 on the 1st by 3-1 in sets and game 2 on the 3rd by 3-2 in sets, finished the series in game 3. Unlike Korea Expressway Corporation, which went straight to the championship as the regular-season first-place team, GS Caltex had played its sixth postseason game on that day. Stamina was a variable. But the in-form GS Caltex squad overcame the physical challenge. They reclaimed the top spot for the first time in five years since the 2020-21 season. GS Caltex lifted their fourth women's professional volleyball championship trophy overall, after the 2007-2008, 2013-2014 and 2020-2021 seasons. 3위에서 시작한 GS Caltex set a record as the first team in history to win the championship having started from the quarterfinals and became the first third-place team to win the title undefeated. title: GS Caltex coach Lee Young-taek shed tears over the joy of winning and gratitude to the players.GS Ca.. - 매일경제
content:
GS Caltex coach Lee Young-taek shed tears over the joy of winning and gratitude to the players. GS Caltex won the third match of the women's championship match between Korea Expressway Corporation and Jin Air 2025-26 V-League at Jangchung Gymnasium on the 5th with a set score of 3-1 (25-15 19-25 25-20 25-16). As a result, GS Caltex became the champion with six consecutive wins in spring volleyball through the semi-playoff, playoff, and championship games. After the 2020-21 season, when the women's first treble (KOVO Cup + regular league + championship match) was achieved, he enjoyed his fourth career victory in the postseason. Coach Lee enjoyed the feat of winning his first championship on the volleyball stage in the first spring of his manager's career. After the game, he said, "It's like a dream. It was a position I wanted to start and achieve as a leader, but I was able to climb thanks to the players. "I'm so grateful," he said. "I keep crying, maybe because I'm old." It's because of our players," he said. GS Caltex's champion road was not smooth. He slowed down at the beginning of the season and ranked fifth with 11 wins and 13 losses in the first half. He rebounded in the second half and finished third in the regular league, and eventually won the championship. Looking back on the season, Lee said, "Of course, I didn't expect it. The first goal was to advance to spring volleyball. The training process was not bad, but there was also time to revise the plan due to the occurrence of injured players during the season. "The regular league was so mixed that both me and the players were stressed out," he said. "We confirmed our advance to the postseason in the final regular league match. Many people around me say that they can make results in short-term matches because there is Silva. I prepared it with the same thought. As expected, Silva showed the best performance. Even when the players were exhausted, they did their best. I held on well and won. It's the result of the players' performance," he said. Ace Silva showed his strength by scoring more than 30 points in six consecutive spring volleyball games. Coach Lee said, "He's a great player," adding, "It's hard to know how to express it. I had knee pain during the third set in today's game. I was sorry I couldn't take you off the court, but you got through it. All I can say is that it's great," he praised. He said, "All the players' skills have improved. Captain Yoo Soo-yeon also had a career high, and Min-ji (Kwon) also showed a sense of stability by going to various positions. Ga-eun (Choi) also played the main role from the fifth round and showed her skills in the postseason. He added, "My skills have improved to the point where it is difficult to pick one." Lee has also grown. Taking the helm in March 2024, he suffered from continued sluggishness last season, but it was amazing to reach the top in one season. Lee said of himself, "He was a poor coach who lost 14 consecutive games last season and escaped from last place. This season, there was no significant change in the squad except for Raina joining. I'm happy to continue the season until the end like this. I'm the same, but the players did a good job," he said, showing humility. GS Caltex, who now has to plan for the next season. Lee's first task seems to be to stay with ace Silva. He said, "There are some FA players. We couldn't talk separately during the postseason. It's time to talk. In particular, I think I need to talk a lot with Silva. I wish you would join us if you don't retire. Last season we couldn't go to spring volleyball and convinced Silva to join us early. I'll talk about it well again this time, he said. "The FA market will open soon. I usually meet with FA players in person. I will persuade him well," he added. title: GS Caltex Wins Undefeated Championship, Silva Named MVP - 조선일보
content: Silva's 36.3 average, 47%+ leads GS Caltex to first title in five years, undefeated GS Caltex won 3-1 (25-15 19-25 25-20 25-20) in the third match of the 2025-2026 V-League Women’s Division Championship Finals held at Jangchung Gymnasium on the 5th. Silva scored 36 points, Kwon Minji 15 points, and Oh Se-yeon 11 points. Silva, who topped the regular season in scoring, continued her strong performance in the postseason. Appearing in all six postseason games, she averaged 36.3 points, proving her worth as the ‘Cuba Express’ and was selected as MVP. She received 33 out of 34 votes (1 abstention) in the press corps poll, effectively a unanimous decision. She scored over 40 points twice and recorded over 30 points in all six games. Additionally, Silva’s attack success rate never dropped below 47%. In the third match, she scored 36 points with a 47.89% attack success rate. GS Caltex, which defeated Heungkuk Life in the quarterfinals and Hyundai Engineering & Construction in the semifinals, advanced to the championship finals without losing a single game to Korea Expressway Corporation. From the quarterfinals to the championship finals, GS Caltex played six games and remained undefeated. On the 24th of last month, they secured a 3-1 victory over Heungkuk Life in the quarterfinals and advanced to the semifinals. In the first semifinal against Hyundai Engineering & Construction on the 25th, they won 3-1, and in the second semifinal on the 28th, they won 3-0, successfully advancing to the finals. They also won all matches against Korea Expressway Corporation. In the first match on the 1st, they won 3-1, and in the second match on the 3rd, they won 3-2, concluding the series in the third match. During this process, Silva scored 42 points with a 59.15% attack success rate against Heungkuk Life. In the first semifinal against Hyundai Engineering & Construction, she scored 40 points (49.65% attack success rate), and in the second semifinal, she scored 32 points (49.09% attack success rate). In the first match against Korea Expressway Corporation on the 1st, she scored 33 points (49.21% attack success rate), and in the second match, she scored 35 points (47.69% attack success rate), leading GS Caltex to an undefeated championship. Unlike Korea Expressway Corporation, which advanced directly to the finals as the regular league leader, GS Caltex played their sixth postseason game up to that day. Physical stamina was a concern, but the GS Caltex team overcame the challenges. They reclaimed the title for the first time in five years since the 2020-2021 season. GS Caltex has now won their fourth championship trophy in the Women’s Professional Volleyball Championship Finals, following their victories in the 2007-2008, 2013-2014, and 2020-2021 seasons. Starting from third place, GS Caltex set a historic record as the first team to win the championship finals after starting in the quarterfinals and the first third-place team to achieve an undefeated championship. [OSEN=Hong Jisu Reporter] GS Caltex, which ranked third in the regular season, achieved an undefeated postseason championship. ‘Cupa Express’ Gisele Silva became the MVP.

title: Third-Place GS Caltex Wins Women’s V-League Championship Undefeated - 조선일보
content: Cuban star Gyselle Silva shatters scoring records, leads historic postseason run The third match of the women’s V-League Championship Final between GS Caltex and Korea Expressway Corporation, held at Jangchung Gymnasium in Seoul on the 5th. Before the game, Gyselle Silva (35), a foreign player for GS Caltex, and her husband and daughter Siana (6) stood on the court. Silva, who had been warming up, smiled warmly and kissed her daughter’s cheek. Once the game began, Silva’s “motherly smile” vanished completely. With a cold gaze, she focused intently on the match, never showing emotion—whether scoring or conceding points. Only after the final point secured her team’s victory in the fourth set did Silva raise her arms and grin brightly. GS Caltex defeated Korea Expressway Corporation 3-1 (25-15, 19-25, 25-20, 25-20) to claim the season’s championship. Ranked fifth when the fifth round began, GS Caltex fought back with eight wins and four losses to finish third in the regular league. They then swept six consecutive postseason matches: a single-game playoff against fourth-place Heungkuk Life, a best-of-three playoff against second-place Hyundai Engineering & Construction, and a best-of-five championship series against first-place Korea Expressway Corporation, completing a perfect “upset victory.” This marked GS Caltex’s first championship in five years since their last integrated title in 2021. A third-place team winning the championship in the women’s pro volleyball league has occurred only four times: GS Caltex in 2008, Heungkuk Life in 2009, Korea Expressway Corporation in 2023, and now GS Caltex this season. However, GS Caltex became the first third-place team to win the postseason undefeated. At the center of the victory was the “Cuban ace” Silva. Despite criticism of GS Caltex’s “all-in volleyball” strategy—jokingly suggested to stand for “Gyselle Silva”—the team trusted her completely, assigning nearly half of their attacks to her. Silva’s regular-season attack share was 43.03%. She played all 36 regular-season games, relentlessly spiking with ironclad stamina, and shattered the single-season scoring record for the women’s division with 1,083 points. In her third year in the domestic league, she also became the first player to score over 1,000 points for three consecutive seasons. Silva’s dominance continued in the postseason. She scored over 30 points in six straight games, finishing the season with 1,301 points across the regular season and playoffs—a new all-time record. She was voted Championship Final MVP by the on-site press corps, receiving 33 out of 34 votes (one abstention). When asked about her future with the team, Silva replied, “It’s not my decision.” GS Caltex Vice Chairman Hur Sae-hong responded, “We promise to renew her contract,” and fans cheered her name in celebration. Domestic players like Kwon Min-ji and Yoo Seo-yeon also supported Silva by sharing offensive responsibilities. Setter Ahn Hye-jin gained stability as the season progressed, laying the foundation for the title. Korea Expressway Corporation, which dominated the regular season as the clear first place, collapsed against GS Caltex. The team’s atmosphere became chaotic after abruptly parting ways with head coach Kim Jong-min, who had led them for a decade, ahead of the championship. Additionally, foreign star Moma Basoko (Cameroon) underperformed, contributing to their three straight losses.

title: GS Caltex Reclaims V-League Title After Five-Year Gap - 조선일보
content: Silva's 36 Points Lead Team to Championship Victory Over Korea Expressway Corporation [OSEN=Hong Ji-su Reporter] GS Caltex, which finished 3rd in the regular season, concluded the season at the highest position. GS Caltex won 3-1 (25-15 19-25 25-20 25-20) in sets in the 3rd match of the 2025-2026 V-League women’s championship decider held at Jangchung Gymnasium on the 5th. They reclaimed the top spot for the first time in five years since the 2020-21 season. Silva scored 36 points, Kwon Min-ji 15 points, and Oh Se-yeon 11 points. Korea Expressway Corporation’s Moma scored 18 points and 15 points, but they were no match for GS Caltex’s firepower. GS Caltex, which defeated Heungkuk Life in the semifinals and Hyundai Engineering & Construction in the playoffs, ascended to the championship decider without losing a single match to Korea Expressway Corporation. GS Caltex, which won 3-1 in the 1st match and 3-2 in the 2nd match on the 3rd, concluded the series in the 3rd match. In the 1st set, GS Caltex took the first score when Korea Expressway Corporation’s Kim Sa-bin’s quick attack went out. Silva’s quick open attack then made it 2-0. Thanks to the performances of Kwon Min-ji and Silva, GS Caltex took the lead. Although they faced a reversal due to Kang So-hwi’s unstoppable attacks, they turned the game around with Oh Se-yeon’s blocking and quick attack, Silva’s quick open attack, and another block from Oh Se-yeon. The momentum continued with Silva and Kwon Min-ji’s performances, shaking off Korea Expressway Corporation’s pursuit. The 2nd set was fiercely contested. Silva’s open attack and blocking gave them an early lead, but they began to trail as they failed to contain Moma, Bae Yu-na, and Thanacha. In the latter half, they fell behind due to Moma and Kim Sa-bin, resulting in a 1-1 set score. In the 3rd set, GS Caltex remained locked in a close battle until Moma’s strong serve and Thanacha’s quick open attack put them at 3-5. However, Kwon Min-ji, Choi Ga-eun, and Silva stepped up to tie it at 6-6. The score reached 8-8, and after Korea Expressway Corporation’s Lee Yoon-jung’s serve went out, Silva’s successful attack made it 10-8. Oh Se-yeon’s block and Kwon Min-ji’s point extended the lead to 12-8. Korea Expressway Corporation’s counterattack led by Thanacha and Moma was fierce, but Silva bombarded them. Silva’s three consecutive points secured the 3rd set for GS Caltex. In the early stages of the 4th set, Kwon Min-ji and Choi Ga-eun shone, while Korea Expressway Corporation countered with Moma and Thanacha. However, Lee Yoon-jung’s unforced error and Moma’s attack out widened the gap. GS Caltex pulled away with points from Silva and Yu Seo-yeon. At 15-11, Yu Seo-yeon’s open attack, block, and quick open attack in succession extended the lead to 18-11. The momentum shifted decisively to GS Caltex. At 20-14, Silva scored, followed by Oh Se-yeon’s block. Although Silva’s attack was blocked by Kang So-hwi and Thanacha, narrowing the gap, Kwon Min-ji’s consecutive points decided the championship decider winner.

title: GS Caltex won its fourth star in its career. He embraced the trophy to the fullest, beating the Kore.. - 매일경제
content:
GS Caltex won its fourth star in its career. He embraced the trophy to the fullest, beating the Korea Expressway Corporation, which ranks first in the regular league. GS Caltex won the third match of the women's championship match between Korea Expressway Corporation and Jin Air 2025-26 V-League at Jangchung Gymnasium on the 5th with a set score of 3-1 (25-15 19-25 25-20 25-16). As a result, GS Caltex, ranked third in the regular league, topped the V-League with its victory in the championship game. Following the semi-playoffs and playoffs, he won the championship with six consecutive wins in spring volleyball. This is the fourth time that the third place in the regular league has won the championship game. After GS Caltex in the 2007-08 season, Heungkuk Life in the 2008-09 season, and Korea Expressway Corporation in the 2022-23 season, GS Caltex became the main character. It is the first time that the third-place team in the regular league has won the championship game. GS Caltex made its fourth career star in spring volleyball, which came back in five years. The last championship was also in the 2021-22 season, when it was the last spring volleyball. At that time, GS Caltex achieved the first treble in the women's division (KOVO Cup + regular league + championship match). On the other hand, the Korea Expressway Corporation went straight to the championship game as the top of the regular league, but ended the season with three losses in three games. Ahead of the championship match, head coach Kim Jong-min left the team due to the expiration of the contract. Coach Kim was recently summarily indicted on charges of assaulting the coach. The news of the breakup was reported in the absence of a court ruling or a disciplinary action from the Korea Volleyball Federation (KOVO). Director Kim's contract period is until the 31st of last month. In response, he sent a request to allow him to lead the team until the championship game, but the club reportedly refused to do so. In the end, the Korea Expressway Corporation was unable to overcome the aftermath of the lack of supervision. He tried to save his memory of winning the reverse sweep in the 2022-23 season, but it was not enough. It failed to break GS Caltex's sharp rise in spring volleyball. For GS Caltex, ace Silva led the team to win. He showed his fighting spirit despite his injury in the third set and scored 36 points. Kwon Min-ji scored 14 points and Oh Se-yeon scored 11. Moma and Tanacha scored 31 points in the Korea Expressway Corporation, but failed to prevent the defeat. GS Caltex dominated the first set. Silva scored 16 points and Kwon Min-ji scored 9 points. Silva and Kwon Min-ji's attacks fired from the beginning of the set. It was tied at 9-9 due to the chase of the Korea Expressway Corporation, but Silva, Oh Se-yeon and Kwon Min-ji tied the score for the sixth consecutive time, bringing the atmosphere. He then scored another six consecutive points at 17-13. After that, ace Silva won the match point and succeeded in gaining the upper hand. In the second set, the Korea Expressway Corporation brought the game to square one. The road corporation has revived Moma's aggression. Bae Yu-na also contributed with four points and a 100% attack success rate. Kim Se-bin made consecutive blockings after Moma's back attack at 18-18. Then, at 24-19, the opponent's serve mistake made the set score 1-1. GS Caltex took the lead again in the third set. Silva, Oh Se-yeon, and Kwon Min-ji took the lead with consecutive points at 8-8. The Korea Expressway Corporation chased Moma and Tanacha, but GS Caltex did not give up the lead. GS Caltex ace Silva even showed off his injury. Attempting to attack at 19-16, complained of discomfort in the left knee area. Nevertheless, he did not let go of his concentration at every important moment. At 22-19, he reached the match point with consecutive openers and even won the last score, claiming to be the main character. In the fourth set, GS Caltex confirmed the championship. He never gave up the lead after leading with an opponent's mistake and Silva's back attack at 4-4. In the middle of the set, Ahn Hye-jin and Silva were replaced to give them a break. At 15-11, GS Caltex's captain Yoo Se-yeon raised the mood with three consecutive points. Oh Se-yeon also added strength to widen the gap. Silva and Ahn Hye-jin were put in again at 20-13. He allowed three points to his opponent at 22-14, but Oh Se-yeon's blocking and opening reached the match point. After that, Kwon Min-ji finished the game with a quick open. title: GS Caltex teams up with France's Veolia on smart, green plant push - The Korea Herald
content:
Business GS Caltex teams up with France's Veolia on smart, green plant push GS Caltex has formed a strategic partnership with France’s Veolia to upgrade utility operations and strengthen sustainability at its Yeosu plant, the company said Sunday. The agreement, signed during French President Emmanuel Macron’s visit to Korea, marks a shift from project-based cooperation to a broader, long-term partnership. Under the deal, the two companies will jointly develop integrated solutions across environmental, energy and digital domains to meet tightening regulations and rising demand for efficiency. The collaboration will be structured under a “Triple-X” framework — business transformation (BX), digital and AI transformation (DAX), and green transformation (GX). In BX, the partners will focus on optimizing wastewater treatment at the Yeosu plant and exploring utility synergies with nearby industrial sites to improve operational efficiency. For DAX, GS Caltex will adopt Veolia’s AI-based solutions to enable real-time data analysis and advanced process control, supporting a shift toward a smart plant through predictive maintenance and anomaly detection. In GX, the companies will deploy eco-friendly technologies, such as wastewater reuse, cooling system optimization and resource recovery to cut emissions and strengthen circular economy practices. “The partnership with Veolia marks a key step in transforming our utility operations and advancing ESG management,” GS Caltex Vice Chairman Hur Sae-hong said. “We aim to build sustainable competitiveness based on world-class technologies and operational expertise.” Trump threatens to strike Iran's infrastructure if Hormuz isn't reopened US President Donald Trump on Sunday stepped up his threat to hit Iran's critical infrastructure hard if the country's government doesn’t reopen the Strait of Hormuz by his Monday deadline. title: Becky G’s See-Through Naked Wardrobe Lace Top Hangs on by a Few Hooks - aol.com
content: Becky G is turning heads with her fashion choices for her latest music video, donning a see-through lace top hanging by a few hooks and a pair of leggings for the “Marathon” MV. The singer wore a bold, head-to-toe red ensemble that perfectly blends street style with high-fashion risk-taking. Known for her fearless approach to trends, Becky G’s newest look is a masterclass in monochrome styling. It captures a daring, modern aesthetic that is already sparking conversations across the fashion world and social media. The centerpiece of Becky G’s striking look is a vibrant red stretch lace cardigan top from Naked Wardrobe. The top features a delicate, see-through floral lace pattern that offers a hint of skin while maintaining a structured silhouette. Adding to the drama, the cardigan is held together by just a few small hooks at the center, creating a daring peekaboo effect. This barely-there fastening gives the outfit a sense of effortless, edgy movement that works perfectly for a music video setting. She paired the top with matching red lace leggings, also from Naked Wardrobe. These leggings carry the same intricate floral motif and sheerness, creating a seamless and bold monochrome aesthetic. To round out the ensemble, Becky G wore red pointed-toe heels with buckle details and carried a sleek black shoulder bag. Her long, dark hair was styled in soft, voluminous waves that draped over her shoulders, adding a touch of classic glamor to the trendy outfit. Becky G looked absolutely radiant and confident while filming on location. Her makeup remained polished with a sharp winged liner and a neutral lip, ensuring the focus stayed on the fiery red attire. The overall look is a perfect representation of her signature style: fearless, feminine, and always ahead of the curve. Originally reported by Samridhi Goel on theFashionSpot. title: GS Caltex Edges Korea Expressway in Five-Set Thriller, Closes in on Title - 조선일보
content: Setter Ahn Hye-jin vows to clinch championship at home amid sold-out third game in Seoul [OSEN=Gimcheon, Reporter Son Chan-ik] “It’s so fun and thrilling.” A single sentence from GS Caltex setter Ahn Hye-jin encapsulated all the emotions of the day’s match. It was a hard-fought victory seized after a cliffhanger battle. GS Caltex won the second game of the Jin Air 2025-2026 V-League Women’s Division Championship Finals against Korea Expressway Corporation on the 3rd at Gimcheon Gymnasium, prevailing in a full five-set thriller. Having taken both the first and second games, GS Caltex now needs only one more win to clinch the championship. Ace Silva delivered a standout performance with 35 points, serving as the key problem-solver. Yu Seo-yeon (11 points) and Reina (10 points) also contributed, completing a balanced attack. After the match, Ahn Hye-jin said, “The serve points were so thrilling, and I finally felt the joy of blocking again after a long time. I was flustered when things didn’t go well early on, but I’m really happy we could win after going to the fifth set,” she smiled. Having advanced to the championship finals through the semifinals and playoffs, the physical burden was significant. However, the players’ morale grew even stronger. Ahn Hye-jin explained, “The atmosphere is so good that the players say, ‘I don’t feel like we’re going to lose.’ There were times during the season when we felt uneasy, but winning continuously has boosted our confidence.” She added, “It would be a lie to say I’m not hurt, but overcoming this makes the joy twice as big.” Now, attention turns to the third game. GS Caltex will have the chance to secure the championship at Seoul’s Jangchung Gymnasium on the 5th. Ahn Hye-jin stated, “I have no desire to return to Gimcheon. I want to finish it at home. I heard tickets are already sold out, so I’ll repay the fans with a great game in front of them,” she vowed. Unlike five years ago, when they waited as the regular league champions, this time they are challenging for the top spot after climbing step by step. Regarding this, Ahn Hye-jin said, “It’s actually better because we can maintain our game sense. The more we play, the less anxious we feel and the more confident we become. Right now, we’re really enjoying it.” Finally, she expressed trust in ace Silva. “She’s truly amazing. Her sense of responsibility and competitive spirit are immense. Even when she must be tired, she keeps asking for the ball. I thought something bad would happen if I didn’t give it to her,” she laughed. She added, “Even in tough situations late in the game, she resolved them, which gave me more confidence.”

title: Key Takeaways From Becky G’s ‘Call Her Daddy’ Interview - HipLatina
content:
Becky G is the latest guest to sit down on Call Her Daddy, and she used her time with host Alex Cooper to get candid about the parts of her story that don’t always make it into headlines. Over the course of the conversation, the singer opened up about growing up as the financial backbone of her family, navigating a complicated relationship with her father, and the emotional weight of being a provider at a young age. She also addressed her very public relationship, speaking for the first time in depth about infidelity, calling off her engagement, and the decision to reconcile—on her own terms. The interview arrives at a time when Becky G is entering a new chapter personally and professionally, but what stood out most wasn’t a career update—it was the clarity. There’s a throughline in how she talks about responsibility, boundaries, and healing, especially as a Latina navigating expectations around family, loyalty, and sacrifice. You can’t do the work for someone else.” “I don’t think our parents go through life saying ‘I want to cause our kids harm;’ these are things that just systematically exist.” “I don’t make decisions passively… I called off my engagement. title: Investors Heavily Search The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc. (GS): Here is What You Need to Know - Yahoo Finance Singapore
content: Tip: Try a valid symbol or a specific company name for relevant results Goldman Sachs (GS) has been one of the most searched-for stocks on Zacks.com lately. So, you might want to look at some of the facts that could shape the stock's performance in the near term. Over the past month, shares of this investment bank have returned +3.3%, compared to the Zacks S&P 500 composite's -4.2% change. During this period, the Zacks Financial - Investment Bank industry, which Goldman falls in, has lost 1.4%. The key question now is: What could be the stock's future direction? Although media reports or rumors about a significant change in a company's business prospects usually cause its stock to trend and lead to an immediate price change, there are always certain fundamental factors that ultimately drive the buy-and-hold decision. Here at Zacks, we prioritize appraising the change in the projection of a company's future earnings over anything else. That's because we believe the present value of its future stream of earnings is what determines the fair value for its stock. We essentially look at how sell-side analysts covering the stock are revising their earnings estimates to reflect the impact of the latest business trends. And if earnings estimates go up for a company, the fair value for its stock goes up. A higher fair value than the current market price drives investors' interest in buying the stock, leading to its price moving higher. This is why empirical research shows a strong correlation between trends in earnings estimate revisions and near-term stock price movements. Goldman is expected to post earnings of $16.48 per share for the current quarter, representing a year-over-year change of +16.7%. Over the last 30 days, the Zacks Consensus Estimate has changed -3.7%. For the current fiscal year, the consensus earnings estimate of $57.86 points to a change of +12.7% from the prior year. Over the last 30 days, this estimate has changed +2.2%. For the next fiscal year, the consensus earnings estimate of $65.19 indicates a change of +12.7% from what Goldman is expected to report a year ago. Over the past month, the estimate has changed +4.1%. Having a strong externally audited track record, our proprietary stock rating tool, the Zacks Rank, offers a more conclusive picture of a stock's price direction in the near term, since it effectively harnesses the power of earnings estimate revisions. Due to the size of the recent change in the consensus estimate, along with three other factors related to earnings estimates, Goldman is rated Zacks Rank #3 (Hold). The chart below shows the evolution of the company's forward 12-month consensus EPS estimate: While earnings growth is arguably the most superior indicator of a company's financial health, nothing happens as such if a business isn't able to grow its revenues. After all, it's nearly impossible for a company to increase its earnings for an extended period without increasing its revenues. So, it's important to know a company's potential revenue growth. For Goldman, the consensus sales estimate for the current quarter of $17.01 billion indicates a year-over-year change of +12.9%. For the current and next fiscal years, $63.18 billion and $66.69 billion estimates indicate +8.4% and +5.6% changes, respectively. Goldman reported revenues of $13.45 billion in the last reported quarter, representing a year-over-year change of -3%. EPS of $14.01 for the same period compares with $11.95 a year ago. Compared to the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $13.61 billion, the reported revenues represent a surprise of -1.14%. The EPS surprise was +19.03%. The company beat consensus EPS estimates in each of the trailing four quarters. The company topped consensus revenue estimates three times over this period. No investment decision can be efficient without considering a stock's valuation. Whether a stock's current price rightly reflects the intrinsic value of the underlying business and the company's growth prospects is an essential determinant of its future price performance. While comparing the current values of a company's valuation multiples, such as price-to-earnings (P/E), price-to-sales (P/S), and price-to-cash flow (P/CF), with its own historical values helps determine whether its stock is fairly valued, overvalued, or undervalued, comparing the company relative to its peers on these parameters gives a good sense of the reasonability of the stock's price. As part of the Zacks Style Scores system, the Zacks Value Style Score (which evaluates both traditional and unconventional valuation metrics) organizes stocks into five groups ranging from A to F (A is better than B; B is better than C; and so on), making it helpful in identifying whether a stock is overvalued, rightly valued, or temporarily undervalued. Goldman is graded B on this front, indicating that it is trading at a discount to its peers. Click here to see the values of some of the valuation metrics that have driven this grade. The facts discussed here and much other information on Zacks.com might help determine whether or not it's worthwhile paying attention to the market buzz about Goldman. However, its Zacks Rank #3 does suggest that it may perform in line with the broader market in the near term. Want the latest recommendations from Zacks Investment Research? title: GS Caltex Wins Second Straight, One Victory from Championship Title - 조선일보
content: Silva's 35 points lead GS Caltex to 3-2 victory; title match set for 5th at home The women’s professional volleyball team GS Caltex defeated Korea Expressway Corporation with a set score of 3-2 (25-15, 14-25, 20-25, 25-22, 15-7) in the V-League Championship Decider held at Gimcheon Gymnasium on the 3rd. Following the first match, they have now won two consecutive games, leaving only one more victory needed to clinch the championship decider title. GS Caltex, which rose to third place by narrowly edging out Heungkuk Life with a better set ratio after a fierce late-season chase in the regular league, went on to win all five "spring volleyball" matches, including the semifinals against Heungkuk Life, the playoffs against Hyundai Engineering & Construction, and the second match of the Championship Decider against regular league champion Korea Expressway Corporation. GS Caltex is challenging for the top spot five years after achieving a combined regular league and Championship Decider title during the 2020-2021 season. They will play the third match at their home stadium, Seoul Jangchung Gymnasium, on the 5th. On this day, the two teams saw a firepower battle between foreign players Gisele Silva of GS Caltex and Moma Basoko of Korea Expressway Corporation. Silva, who led her team to victory with 35 points, secured a decisive win over Basoko, who scored 30 points. Basoko’s attack success rate dropped significantly in the fifth set due to fatigue. Silva’s attack success rate was 47.69%, while Basoko’s was 38.57%.

title: GS Caltex Wins Five-Set Match, Title Near - 조선일보
content: GS Caltex needs one more win; Korea Expressway coach addresses morale GS Caltex has also taken the 2nd match of the Championship Finals. GS Caltex defeated Korea Expressway Corporation in a full five sets at the 2nd match of the Jin Air 2025-2026 V-League Championship Finals held at Kimcheon Gymnasium on the 3rd. With this, they now need 1 more win to claim the title. GS Caltex's head coach Lee Young-taek, who secured the first win, stated that rather than making special changes, he would focus on maintaining the existing performance. Coach Lee Young-taek said, “Since we have faced each other many times in the regular league, it is important to focus on what we can do well rather than preparing something new.” He also emphasized, “Although Silva is in a physically tough situation, she is holding up well. Now, the players' mental strength is crucial.” Korea Expressway Corporation’s acting head coach Kim Young-rae, who met with reporters before the match, said, “In the first match, I was in such a mental state that I couldn’t give any instructions. That was the most difficult part.” He added, “I was torn between whether to follow my own style or maintain the coach’s style. During timeouts, I remember repeating only the words, ‘It’s okay.’” He continued, “Senior players like Bae Yuna helped set the atmosphere well. A mood formed that ‘we can’t end it like this’ and ‘we must win at all costs.’” He added, “I also put aside my apologetic feelings and talked about starting over with a sense of responsibility.” GS Caltex took the 1st set. Leading with Silva and Yoo Seo-yeon, who combined for 13 points, they pressed on with a 10-point difference, 25-15. Korea Expressway Corporation, launching a counterattack, began to gain momentum in the 2nd set and finished with 25-14. Riding the momentum, Korea Expressway Corporation shook off GS Caltex’s persistent chase in the 3rd set and won 25-20. The strength of the team that secured the first win was formidable. GS Caltex, who took the 4th set in a close battle 25-22, neatly concluded the 5th set. /what@osen.co.kr

title: Thermo Fisher Scientific Launches Gibco CHOvantage GS Cell Line Development Kit To Accelerate Biologics Development - BioPharma APAC
content: Gibco™ CHOvantage™ GS Cell Line Development Kit delivers high productivity, streamlined timelines and royalty-free, clinical stage licensing options to support scalable manufacturing. Gibco CHOvantage GS Cell Line Development Kit delivers high productivity, streamlined timelines and royalty-free, clinical-stage licensing options to support scalable manufacturing Thermo Fisher Scientific Inc., the world leader in serving science,  announced a next-generation, integrated cell line development platform that enables biologics developers to accelerate time to clinic while maintaining regulatory confidence and commercial scalability. Global pipelines for protein therapeutics, including complex biologics and biosimilars used to treat conditions ranging from cancer to autoimmune and rare diseases, continue to expand. This growth is placing increasing pressure on manufacturers to shorten time to clinic, improve manufacturing consistency and control cost of goods without compromising quality or regulatory compliance. At the same time, regulatory agencies are placing greater emphasis on demonstrating product quality and comparability to innovator molecules. The Gibco™ CHOvantage™ GS Cell Line Development (CLD) Kit enables researchers to generate high-performing, CHO cell lines, achieving protein titers of ≥7 g/L in fed-batch cultures, establishing stable pools in as little as four weeks, and supporting selection of stable clones within 14 weeks, helping to streamline progression from early development to commercial manufacturing. “Biologics developers face increasing pressure to deliver therapies faster while managing cost, quality and regulatory complexity,” said Sara Henneman, vice president and general manager of Thermo Fisher Scientific’s cell culture and cell therapy business. “With the CHOvantage GS CLD Kit, we’re helping customers compress development timelines, increase confidence in quality and lower overall costs through a royalty-free, clinical-stage licensing model that helps minimize barriers to market. This platform reflects our commitment to simplifying and standardizing biologics development workflows from research through commercial production.” Patent expirations are accelerating the development of lower-cost biosimilar medicines, increasing demand for reliable and scalable technologies that can help manufacturers bring these treatments to market more quickly while maintaining strict quality and regulatory standards. Integrated development platforms can help simplify complex manufacturing processes, shorten development timelines and support consistent large-scale production. “The CHOvantage GS CLD Kit is our newest example of how we are advancing cell line productivity and process consistency across the biologics lifecycle,” said Andy Campbell, senior director of research and development at Thermo Fisher Scientific. “By integrating a transposon-based vector system with our Efficient-Pro media and feeds, we’re giving customers a reliable, high-quality, streamlined platform that helps to save time, reduces variability and supports scalability. Importantly, this harmonized workflow is designed to simplify tech transfer and enable a smoother transition from development to manufacturing.” BioFactura (a developer and manufacturer of high-value biosimilars and novel drugs) was a key pre-launch testing partner for the Gibco CHOvantage GS Cell Line Development Kit. Lead Cell Line Development Scientist Carson Brackna stated, "Combining transposon vectors and optimized media and feeds, the CHOvantage platform offered a robust, end-to-end cell line package while remaining user-friendly and yielding quality protein with high productivity on a tight schedule.” Simplified Licensing, No Clinical-Stage RoyaltiesMany cell line development systems require royalties or milestone payments during the clinical stage. The CHOvantage GS CLD Kit includes research-use rights with purchase, avoiding these common fees during development. Royalty-free, clinical-stage licensing options provide greater cost predictability and commercial flexibility, enabling a more seamless transition from development through full-scale production.
